site.title: MoS-T — Spatio-Temporal Patterns
site.description: ANR Project MoS-T (MoST): Mining and visualization of frequent spatio-temporal patterns in large graphs.

hero.subtitle: Spatio-Temporal Patterns
hero.description: ANR Project (Oct. 2021 – June 2026) — Mining and visualization of frequent spatio-temporal patterns in large graphs.

home.about_text: The MoS-T project aims to discover and visualize frequent spatio-temporal patterns in large graphs. The goal is to develop innovative methods combining graph mining and deep neural networks to extract meaningful patterns and provide synthetic, interpretable representations.

research.title: Research
objectives: Propose graph models to represent spatio-temporal relationships in data
Develop GNN architectures tailored to spatio-temporal series
Validate the proposed approaches on real-world use cases (environment, health, transport)
research.wp1_desc: Development of methods to automatically construct graphs representing spatial and temporal relationships in data.
research.wp2_desc: Design of GNN architectures tailored to processing spatio-temporal series structured as graphs.
research.wp3_desc: Validation of the approaches on real use cases in the fields of environment, health, and transport.

comic.title: The project in comics
comic.description: Discover the MoS-T project through a scientific outreach comic strip.
